channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Why Graph-added People Skills Don’t Show Up on the User Profile Card
item.description: Microsoft’s People Skills service can use AI to attribute 16,000 different skills to users. Those skills show up on the user profile card. However, once a Microsoft 365 tenant enables People Skills, they lose the ability to add custom skills for user profiles via Graph APIs or People Connectors....
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Public Preview Update: Azure Copilot Observability Agent
item.description: Modern cloud applications generate massive amounts of telemetry - metrics, logs, traces, alerts, and platform signals. Yet whether you're asking questions about your observability data or responding when things go wrong, discovering insights and root causes requires a deep understanding of the...
item.dc:creator: EfratNauerman

item.title: Platform Improvements for Python AI Apps on Azure App Service
item.description: Overview Azure App Service (Linux) is a fully managed PaaS offering that supports a broad range of languages, including Python, Node.js, .NET, PHP, and Java. Developers can push source code or deploy a pre-built artifact; the platform handles the rest, including dependency installation, application...
item.dc:creator: SurenderSinghMalik

item.title: WebAssembly Data Processing at the Edge with Azure IoT Operations
item.description: Safe, composable dataflow operators for Azure IoT Operations, built as WASM modules using the Component Model, WIT interfaces, and WASI Preview 2. The post WebAssembly Data Processing at the Edge with Azure IoT Operations appeared first on ISE Developer Blog.
item.dc:creator: Marcel Bindseil

item.title: ﻿﻿Genie in a Bot: Databricks AI/BI Meets Microsoft Teams
item.description: The Use Case: Why Genie Needs to Live in Teams Every organization has business users who need data answers — fast. Marketing managers want to know which campaign drove the most conversions last quarter. Finance teams need spend breakdowns by channel. Executives want real-time KPIs before a board...
item.dc:creator: vikas_gautam

item.title: Control runtime patch updates with Platform Release Channel on Azure App Service for Linux
item.description: Azure App Service for Linux is introducing Platform Release Channel, a new setting that gives you more control over when runtime patch updates are applied to your app. With this feature, you can choose how quickly your app moves to newly rolled-out runtime patches. This helps teams balance two...
item.dc:creator: TulikaC

item.title: Secure Medallion Architecture Pattern on Azure Databricks (Part II)
item.description: Disclaimer: The views in this article are my own and do not represent Microsoft or Databricks. This article is part of a series focused on deploying a secure Medallion Architecture. The series follows a top-down approach , beginning with a high-level architectural perspective and gradually drilling...
item.dc:creator: mscagliola

item.title: Give Your AI Agent Eyes: Browser-Harness Meets Playwright Workspaces Remote Browsers
item.description: What happens when you hand a coding agent a real browser — not a mock, not an API wrapper, but a full Chromium instance running in the cloud? It fills form for you. It does research for you. It navigates JavaScript-heavy SPAs that would make any REST-based scraper weep. And it does it across 10+...
item.dc:creator: NandiniMuralidharan

item.title: PyCon US 2026
item.description: PyCon US 2026 The post PyCon US 2026 appeared first on Microsoft for Python Developers Blog.
item.dc:creator: Vedha Ranganathan

item.title: Data Agent Now Supports Eventhouse Functions, Materialized Views, and Shortcuts (Preview)
item.description: Author: Shreyas Canchi Radhakrishna - Product Manager
item.dc:creator: msft-shreyas

item.title: What's new in FinOps toolkit 14 – April 2026
item.description: FinOps toolkit 14 connects AI to your FinOps hub data with a Copilot Studio agent template, ingests recommendations from Azure Advisor and your own Resource Graph queries, simplifies hub deployment, and previews a new commitment discount eligibility dataset. Whether you consider yourself a FinOps...
item.dc:creator: Michael_Flanakin

item.title: Power BI April 2026 Feature Summary
item.description: Welcome to the April Power BI update!Power BI’s April 2026 update is here, bringing continued improvements across Copilot and AI, reporting, visuals, and modeling. This release includes more flexibility when working with layouts and visuals, expanded Copilot experiences—especially on mobile—and...
item.dc:creator: kamurray

item.title: Microsoft SQL Security Across the MAESTRO Stack: Building Secure Agentic AI with Defense-in-Depth
item.description: Artificial Intelligence is evolving rapidly. What began as simple prompt-and-response systems is now transforming into fully autonomous, agentic AI architectures capable of reasoning, orchestrating tools, interacting with enterprise data, and invoking external systems dynamically. While these...
item.dc:creator: Arun Vijayraghavan

item.title: Process API Improvements in .NET 11
item.description: Process API improvements in .NET 11, including high-level APIs for starting processes, capturing output without deadlocks, handle inheritance control, lifetime management features, and a lightweight `SafeProcessHandle`-based API surface. The post Process API Improvements in .NET 11 appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Adam Sitnik

item.title: Announcing the General Availability of the Next Generation of Azure Boost
item.description: Starting May 7th, 2026, the new Esv7, Dsv7, and Dlsv7 virtual machines are generally available — and underneath them is a fundamentally new generation of Azure Boost. Not an incremental refresh. A platform that took over five years to build, with custom ASIC-hardened logic, a new network adapter,...
item.dc:creator: Max_Uritsky

item.title: From Pipelines to Agents: Self-Healing CI/CD Workflow
item.description: The Brain of the Operation: Azure OpenAI. When building a DevOps agent, following are the points which can be considered to select Azure OpenAI as the ideal choice for logical engine: Native Tool Use: It is specifically optimized for function calling, allowing the agent to interact with Azure...
item.dc:creator: RavinderGupta

item.title: New enterprise installation API now in public preview
item.description: We’re filling an API gap for GitHub App developers based on community feedback. Enterprise Installation API We’ve added a new API that lets a GitHub App find out if it’s… The post New enterprise installation API now in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Service Principal Support for Data Agents in Fabric (Preview)
item.description: Amir Jafari - Principal Product ManagerJoanne Wong - Senior Product Marketing Manager
item.dc:creator: AmirJafari

item.title: Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API
item.description: Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can programmatically start Copilot cloud agent tasks with the new Agent tasks REST API, available in public preview. Copilot cloud agent works in the… The post Start Copilot cloud agent tasks via the REST API appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing Azure Container Apps Express!
item.description: Three years ago, a 15-second cold start was industry-leading. Today, developers and AI agents expect sub-second. The speed bar has moved, and the tooling needs to move with it. After running Azure Container Apps for years, we've learned something important: for most developers, the ACA environment...
item.dc:creator: simonjj

item.title: Announcing the general availability of Azure Integrated Hardware Security Module
item.description: Today we are excited to release general availability for Azure Integrated HSM for AMD v7 Virtual Machines. Azure Integrated HSM is a hardware security module (HSM) cache and crypto offload designed to enhance the security and performance of cryptographic operations in virtual machines. For...
item.dc:creator: simranparkhe

item.title: .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11
item.description: .NET MAUI apps on Android, iOS, and Mac Catalyst now run on CoreCLR by default in .NET 11, unifying the runtime across all of .NET. The post .NET MAUI Moves to CoreCLR in .NET 11 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Ortinau

item.title: Fabric Eventstream can now publish Business Events (Preview)
item.description: Author: Roberto Cervantes Rivero - Senior Product Manager
item.dc:creator: robece-msft

item.title: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) 3.21 enhances deployment efficiency, monitoring capabilities, code security, and policy management. Here are a few highlights in the 3.21 release: Organization custom properties are now generally… The post GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 release candidate is available...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Why your database schema belongs in Git
item.description: Author: Iqra Shaikh - Product Manager 2
item.dc:creator: iqrashaikh

item.title: Agent Skills in Visual Studio: Teach Copilot How Your Team Works
item.description: Visual Studio now supports Agent Skills, which are reusable instruction sets that teach Copilot agents how to handle specific tasks like running a build pipeline, generating boilerplate, or following your team’s coding standards. Define a skill once, and the agent applies it automatically whenever...
item.dc:creator: Simona Liao

item.title: Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them)
item.description: Roguelikes don’t die. They fork, mutate, get argued over, rewritten, abandoned, and revived again. Sometimes all at once. The post Dungeons & Desktops: 10 roguelikes that never die (because their communities won’t let them) appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Lee Reilly

item.title: Encryption in Microsoft 365 – How It Protects Your Data
item.description: In today’s digital workplace, data is constantly moving between emails, cloud storage, collaboration tools, mobile devices, and remote teams. As businesses rely more heavily on cloud platforms, protecting sensitive information has become one of the top priorities for organizations of every size....
item.dc:creator: JohnNaguib

item.title: MS InTune - packaging Amazon DCV client
item.description: Hi,I used the InTune prep tool to bundle the Amazon DCV client. Everything seems to work correctly, bundle created and it uploads well. When I use the company portal to install, it looks like it pushes\installs properly but the DCV client does not run on the laptop after install. This is a .msi...
item.dc:creator: learnazure_ad

item.title: General Availability of SharePoint Framework 1.23 – Advancing the modern developer experience
item.description: We are excited to announce general availability for the SharePoint Framework 1.23. We are rolling out new features in extensions, preview of new CLI and addressing known npm audit issues. The post General Availability of SharePoint Framework 1.23 – Advancing the modern developer experience appeared...
item.dc:creator: Vesa Juvonen

item.title: How to Test AI Agents with LangSmith: A Complete Guide
item.description: Testing AI agents is crucial for ensuring reliability and accuracy in production.Evaluation is a technique to evaluate your agents. Different type of evaluation are # Evaluation Type 1 Task Success (Pass / Fail) 2 Instruction Adherence 3 Correctness / Accuracy 4 Relevance 5 Groundedness...
item.dc:creator: syedarshad

item.title: Understanding How Graph Permissions for Groups Work
item.description: Understanding the set of Graph permissions required by apps can be challenging. In this article we discuss how the Group.Read.All and GroupMember.Real.All permissions work and why you should choose one permission over the other so that you respect the principle of least permission when it comes to...
item.dc:creator: TonyRedmond

item.title: Retrieving the “Device inventory” of iOS devices via the Graph API
item.description: We use Microsoft Intune to manage our iOS mobile devices.To achieve the highest possible level of efficiency, we use PowerShell as a supplementary tool for administration.Since our devices may contain two SIM cards, it is important for us to be able to read this information in order to perform...
item.dc:creator: ATroester

item.title: Interpreter‑Focused Dummy Agent Proposal
item.description: Here's a suggestion for the Windows Insider Program.Tailor Windows Insider Program for the following suggestion. Interpreter‑Focused Dummy Agent Proposal Title: Request for a Sandboxed Agent Container With Support for User‑Defined Interpreters Proposal:As Windows transitions toward an agent‑centric...
item.dc:creator: irwin1050

item.title: Introducing cert-manager for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes: now in Public Preview
item.description: Today we’re releasing a public preview of cert-manager for Azure Arc-enabled Kubernetes. It’s an Arc extension that automates TLS certificate and trust bundle management for edge Kubernetes clusters. If you’re running Kubernetes at the edge: in factories, retail stores, remote sites, you’ve...
item.dc:creator: NicolasMondada

item.title: Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs generation
item.description: What if you could generate realistic attack telemetry on demand? Explore research methods that translate attacker behaviors (TTPs) into synthetic logs that can trigger detections at scale and without sensitive data. The post Accelerating detection engineering using AI-assisted synthetic attack logs...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available!
item.description: Find out about the new features in .NET 11 Preview 4 across the .NET runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, and more! The post .NET 11 Preview 4 is now available! appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: .NET Team

item.title: Fabric April 2026 Feature Summary

item.title: Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark
item.description: Today Microsoft is announcing a major step forward in AI-powered cyber defense: a new multi-model agentic scanning harness (codenamed MDASH). The post Defense at AI speed: Microsoft’s new multi-model agentic security system tops leading industry benchmark appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Taesoo Kim

item.title: Build Smarter, Simpler IoT Messaging with Azure Event Grid MQTT Broker
item.description: Why this matters for modern IoT solutions As IoT solutions grow, teams need messaging that is reliable, scalable, and simple enough to operate across devices, apps, and cloud services. Azure Event Grid MQTT broker helps organizations connect devices and applications using standard MQTT patterns...
item.dc:creator: Connected-Seth

item.title: Company Portal No Longer Installing During Autopilot Enrollment
item.description: Up until today, Autopilot enrollment which included Company Portal from the Microsoft Store (NEW) was successful. Starting today, the same enrollment workflow with similar hardware is failing to install Company Portal, reporting an error code of 0x87D1041C ("The application was not detected after...
item.dc:creator: dsorcinelli_insight

item.title: Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements
item.description: Copilot code review comments are now easier to scan and act on. Available to all users opted into the new pull requests experience, grouped suggestions, severity levels, and an updated… The post Copilot code review: Comment experience improvements appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing
item.description: Starting today, you can download your usage report to see how April’s GitHub Copilot activity translates into AI credits. The new billing unit of AI credits is going live on… The post April reports are now available to prepare for usage-based billing appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description: A recap of the latest servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework for May 2026. The post .NET and .NET Framework May 2026 servicing releases updates appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Rahul Bhandari (MSFT), Tara Overfield

item.title: From Prompt to Production: Open in VS Code for Terraform in Azure Copilot
item.description: We’re excited to introduce a new step in the Terraform on Azure experience: Open in VS Code, now available directly from Azure Copilot in the Azure Portal. This capability helps you move seamlessly from AI‑generated Terraform code to real Azure deployments - within a connected, guided workflow...
item.dc:creator: Jingwei_Wang

item.title: CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more
item.description: CodeQL is the static analysis engine behind GitHub code scanning, which finds and remediates security issues in your code. We’ve recently released CodeQL 2.25.4, which adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improves… The post CodeQL 2.25.4 adds Swift 6.3.1 support, improvements to C# and Java, and more appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot individual plans: Introducing flex allotments in Pro and Pro+, and a new Max plan

item.title: No Exchange Server Security Updates for May 2026
item.description: We wanted to let the Exchange Server community know that there are no security releases for any version of Exchange Server in May 2026, for customers with Exchange SE, or Exchange 2016 or 2019 ESU. Please keep upgrading your organizations to Exchange SE. The Exchange Team
item.dc:creator: The_Exchange_Team
